finding: tp resume derives the spec from the resolved task file's `spec` field, but the spec never defines what happens when discovery finds NO task file and no `<spec>` argument was given. Phase-detection rules 2-5 (§4.3) all presuppose a task file exists; rule 1 needs a spec path to call `tp review --status`. With neither a task file nor a `<spec>` arg there is no spec to detect against — the exit code and hint for this dead end are unspecified.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State the resolution order explicitly (e.g. `<spec>` arg > task-file `spec` field; task file via discovery > spec-adjacent `<base>.tasks.json`) and define the error when nothing resolves: which exit code (3=file?) and hint (`no task file or spec found; run tp init <spec>`).

finding: The §4.2 output object has exactly five fields (phase, spec, tree_clean, next_action, blockers) with no field for the changed/dirty paths. Yet §4.5.1 says `the changed paths are listed` and §11.3 asserts tp resume `lists the changed paths`. There is no schema slot for a path list — an implementer cannot tell whether paths belong in a new top-level array (e.g. `dirty_paths`), inside `blockers` strings, or inside `next_action.payload`.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Add an explicit field to §4.2 (e.g. `dirty_paths: []string`, empty when tree_clean) and reference it from §4.5.1 and §11.3, so the path list has one defined home.

finding: blockers is "an array of strings" but only one literal is pinned anywhere: "dirty working tree" (§11.3). §4.2/§4.5 also route an "incomplete checkpoint" and (via §4.4.3/§11.2) "unmet dependencies" into blockers, yet no exact string is given for either. Two engineers emit different strings (e.g. "incomplete checkpoint" vs "unrecorded round"; "blocked by X,Y" vs "unmet deps: X, Y"), so no deterministic assertion can be written for those blockers.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Pin the exact blocker string literal (and its interpolation format) for each condition — dirty tree, incomplete checkpoint, unmet dependencies — and assert each verbatim in §11.

finding: tree_clean is "whether git status reports a clean working tree" but never defines what counts as dirty. Untracked files (git status shows them; `git status --porcelain` emits `??`), staged-but-uncommitted changes, and ignored files are all unspecified. Two engineers write contradictory tests: one treats a brand-new untracked file as tree_clean:false, another as true. This directly undermines the §11.3/§11.8 assertions, and matters because tp's own dogfood repeatedly has an untracked spec/<version>.md file present at checkpoint boundaries.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Pin tree_clean explicitly: e.g. "tree_clean is true iff `git status --porcelain` produces zero lines (untracked and staged changes both count as dirty; .gitignored paths never count)". Add an acceptance sub-case for an untracked-only working tree.

finding: The whole point of next_action.payload is machine consumption by the next agent, yet the review/audit payload's sub-field names are never pinned. §4.4.1/§4.4.4 and §11.2 say the payload "carries the next round number and unresolved-finding count and the tp review/tp audit command" but give no keys (round vs next_round? unresolved_count vs unresolved? command?). Only the implement payload's keys are pinned ({id, acceptance}, §4.4.3). Two engineers produce incompatible JSON shapes.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Enumerate the exact JSON keys of the review/audit payload (and the decompose/release payloads) the same way §4.4.3 pins {id, acceptance}, and assert them by name in §11.2.

finding: `next_action.payload` is described only in prose per phase (§4.4), never as concrete JSON keys. The review/audit payload must carry `the round number that will be recorded next, the count of unresolved findings, and the tp review invocation` — but the field names (round? next_round? unresolved? unresolved_findings? command?) are never given. §11.2 repeats the prose without keys. The implement payload is pinned to `{id, acceptance}` but the review/audit/decompose/release payloads are not, so their exact object shapes must be invented.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Give each phase's payload an explicit JSON object schema with named keys and types (e.g. review -> {round:int, unresolved:int, command:string}), matching the precision already used for the implement payload's {id, acceptance}.

finding: §4.4.3 and §11.2 define the implement payload as `exactly what tp next --peek --minimal reports it: the WIP task if one exists, otherwise the first ready task`, and §4.5.2 relies on `the existing tp next WIP behavior`. But `tp next --peek` explicitly IGNORES WIP (internal/cli/next.go L54-55: "If --peek, always show next ready (ignore WIP)"). --peek surfaces the first ready task, never the WIP task. So the payload cannot be both `tp next --peek --minimal` output AND surface the WIP task — the two contradict.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Resolve the contradiction: either (a) specify that resume implements WIP-first logic itself (WIP resume, else first ready) and does NOT literally equal `tp next --peek`, or (b) change the reference to non-peek `tp next` semantics; and reconcile §4.5.2's "resume, not a new claim" with the fact that resume is read-only (§4.1) and must not claim.

finding: §5.4 says under an effective hc strategy `tp done --commit <sha> ... is the sole closing path`, implying a bare `tp done <id> "reason"` (no --commit) is rejected. But §3.1 and §7.3 explicitly allow a checkpoint/close with a still-dirty tree (close exits 0, warns), and pre-0.28.0 `tp done <id> "reason"` closes without any commit. Whether hc strategy makes --commit MANDATORY on close — and if so the exit code/hint for a bare close — is not stated, contradicting the surface-don't-force principle.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State whether a bare `tp done` (no commit recorded) is allowed under hc: if it is, `sole closing path` should read `the only commit-recording path`; if it is rejected, give the exit code (2?) and hint. Reconcile with §3.1/§7.3's warn-not-block stance.

finding: The reference-driver loop says "call tp resume; if blockers is non-empty, clear them; otherwise run next_action", i.e. every blocker must be cleared before the loop proceeds. But §3.1, §4.2.5, §7.2 and §10.5 establish that a dirty-tree blocker caused by unrelated changes a project deliberately keeps uncommitted is legitimate, must NOT be committed or discarded, and surfacing it "does not block" the reset. Under §8.2 the driver can neither clear such a blocker (that would force an illegal commit/discard, violating §7.2/§10.5) nor proceed (blockers is non-empty), so it deadlocks or forces the very sweep the design forbids.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Split blockers into must-resolve conditions (incomplete checkpoint: unrecorded round, wip-with-no-commit) versus acknowledge-and-proceed conditions (intentional dirty tree). Have the driver clear only the former and let the operator acknowledge the latter and proceed; or add an `acknowledged`/`hard` flag on each blocker so §8.2's "clear them" applies only to hard blockers.

finding: §3.1 promises that a reset taken with a unit's work uncommitted is "an incomplete checkpoint and is recovered on the next tp resume (§4.5), never silently lost." But §4.5 explicitly states resume "reports, it does not repair, sweep, or block," and its only durable-state recovery signals are §4.5.2 (a wip task with no recorded commit) and §4.5.3 (loose per-role NDJSON with no recorded round). A task already closed done whose diff was left partially uncommitted matches neither signal — the phase has advanced past it (§4.3 rule 3→4) and it shows only as a generic tree_clean:false. So the guaranteed "recovered ... never silently lost" is not delivered for that case, and the word "recovered" contradicts §4.5's "does not repair."

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Weaken §3.1 to say resume "surfaces" (not "recovers") the incomplete checkpoint, and state explicitly that recovery is the agent's action on that surfaced signal. Additionally note the limitation that a done task's forgotten uncommitted work is only surfaced as a dirty tree, not re-associated with the finished unit, so the operator must reconcile it.

finding: §3.4 argues "The quality gate runs at tp done/tp close. Every implementation-task and audit-round checkpoint is therefore a green-gate state." The inference does not hold for audit rounds: the gate runs at task close (tp done/tp close), not at an audit-round boundary, which merges/records findings to .tp-review/. During the audit loop, fixing an audit finding changes code; unless every such fix is routed through a gated tp done/close, an audit-round checkpoint is not guaranteed green. The "therefore" conflates task checkpoints (gated) with audit-round checkpoints (not independently gated).

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Restate §3.4 to say audit-round checkpoints are green only because code is frozen since the last gated task close, and specify how audit-driven code fixes are gated (e.g. via reopen→fix→tp done, or a new gated task) so the green-gate claim is actually enforced rather than assumed.

finding: `tp resume <spec>` accepts an optional spec argument AND derives a spec from the task file's `spec` field. When both are present and disagree (arg spec != task-file spec), the spec does not say which wins, nor whether the task file is still discovered by the normal chain or the spec-adjacent `<base>.tasks.json` is used instead (as ResolveWorkflow already does internally). An implementer must guess the precedence.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Pin the precedence for both the spec source and the task-file source when `<spec>` is supplied, mirroring engine.ResolveWorkflow's documented fallback (discovered file whose `spec` resolves to the arg, else spec-adjacent tasks.json).

finding: §4.1 accepts an optional positional `tp resume <spec>` but in the same sentence says the command "derives the spec path from the task file's `spec` field". The interaction is unspecified: when the positional <spec> is given and differs from the task file's spec field, which wins, and does the positional override task-file discovery of the .tp-review/ state too? No test in §11 covers the positional-argument form at all.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State the precedence of the positional <spec> versus the derived spec (and whether it changes task-file/.tp-review discovery), and add an acceptance case.

finding: §4.1 relies on discovering an active task file and deriving spec from its `spec` field, but defines no behavior when discovery finds no task file, or when the task file's `spec` field is empty/missing. §11.9 only claims "exits 0 in every reachable phase" — the no-task-file / no-spec case is not a phase, so its exit code and output are undefined. This is a common real invocation (running `tp resume` before `tp init`).

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Specify the exit code (likely 3, file, per the conventions) and error output when no task file is discovered or the spec field cannot be resolved, and add an acceptance row.

finding: §4.1 says resume "resolves the active task file ... derives the spec path from the task file's spec field" yet also accepts an optional `tp resume <spec>` argument. Two cases are left undefined: (a) precedence when both the discovered task file's spec field and an explicit <spec> arg are present and differ; (b) behavior when discovery finds no task file at all (pre-init — the driver's very first iteration in §8.2 calls tp resume before any tp init), where there is no spec field to derive from and no phase applies. §11.9 promises resume "exits 0 in every reachable phase," but pre-init is not a phase, so its exit code (presumably 3=file) and message are unspecified.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Define the precedence of an explicit `tp resume <spec>` over the task-file spec field, and specify resume's behavior and exit code when no task file (and no spec arg) can be resolved — e.g. exit 3 with a hint to run `tp init <spec>` first.

finding: The dirty-tree condition is emitted as three things for one fact: tree_clean:false (§4.2.3), a 'dirty working tree' blocker string (§4.2.5, mandated by §11.3), and the changed-path list. The blocker string is fully derivable from tree_clean, so the agent parses two representations of the identical state on every dirty-tree resume.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Drop the derivable 'dirty working tree' blocker; let tree_clean:false plus the path list be the single signal, reserving blockers[] for conditions not already encoded by a typed field. Alternatively drop the tree_clean boolean and derive cleanliness from an empty vs non-empty path list.

finding: `tree_clean` = `whether git status reports a clean working tree` (§4.2.3), but two edge cases are undefined: (1) not a git repo — `git status` errors; §11.9 requires resume to `exit 0 in every reachable phase`, yet the git failure path/exit code and the value of tree_clean are unspecified; (2) untracked files — a fresh `spec/0.28.0.md` or new `.tp-review/` files are untracked; whether untracked content makes tree_clean false (git status --porcelain includes them by default) is not stated, which changes whether nearly every review-phase run reports a dirty tree.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Define tree_clean when git is absent/errors (e.g. tree_clean=null or true with an `unknown-git` note, still exit 0) and state explicitly whether untracked files count toward `clean` (recommend documenting the exact porcelain semantics).

finding: next_action.command is "the literal next tp command to run" (§4.2.4), but its value is unpinned for several phases. For release, §4.4.5 says "no automatic action" — is command then null, "", or a checklist reference string? For implement, the payload is {id, acceptance} but the agent's next step is writing code, not a tp command — is command `tp next`, `tp claim <id>`, or empty? A tester cannot assert next_action.command deterministically per phase.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State the exact command value (or null) for each of the five phases, especially release (no-action) and implement.

finding: §4 never defines --compact behavior for the resume object, yet the schema emits a human-facing next_action.summary gloss (§4.2.4) the agent does not branch on (it acts on command+payload). Under P3 every emitted byte costs context, and tp's --compact contract strips exactly such human-only fields elsewhere, but resume leaves summary unstripped and the compact behavior unspecified.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Specify --compact for tp resume in §4.2: strip next_action.summary (and any TTY-only prose) under compact/piped JSON, keeping phase, spec, tree_clean, next_action.command, next_action.payload, and blockers.

finding: §4.2.4 defines next_action.command as "the literal next tp command to run." Two phases have no such command. In release (§4.4.5) the next step is manual (tag/push/gh release) with "no automatic action" — there is no tp command. In decompose (§4.4.2) the next step is a reasoning task (author tasks) that cannot yet be expressed as a runnable command; tp import is only runnable after the agent has produced the task JSON, so putting `tp import` in command is misleading. The required command field is therefore unsatisfiable/misleading in two of the five phases.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Specify command's value for the non-command phases explicitly — e.g. null (or empty) with the human-facing guidance carried in summary/payload — and state that decompose's command is the eventual `tp import <file>` only as a reminder, not an immediately-runnable command.

finding: Phase detection is first-match on ordered rules (§4.3) with rule 1 = `tp review --status reports converged:false → review`. The spec is silent on staleness: `tp review --status` also reports `stale`, and if the spec is edited after review converged (spec-hash changes), a mid-implementation project could flip converged->false and thus revert phase from `implement` back to `review`. §4.3 does not say whether stale-but-previously-converged counts as `not converged` for rule 1, so an implementer cannot tell if editing the spec during implementation resets the phase.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State how staleness interacts with rule 1 (e.g. rule 1 uses the raw `converged` flag, and a stale converged spec IS treated as unconverged -> back to review), or add an explicit clause exempting phases past decomposition.

finding: Rule 1 pins the review-convergence source precisely (`tp review --status` reports `converged: false`) and the final paragraph clarifies the no-`.tp-review/`-state case resolves to review. Rules 4 and 5 give no such precision for audit: the command/flag that determines "the audit sequence is not converged" is unnamed, and the analog of the no-state clarification (audit never started / no audit rounds recorded) is missing — is a zero-round audit sequence "not converged" (→ audit) or vacuously converged (→ release)? Untestable as written.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Name the audit convergence source (e.g. `tp audit --status` `converged`) symmetric to rule 1, and add a sentence stating a spec with tasks all done and no audit `.tp-review/` state resolves to audit by rule 4.

finding: §4.3 asserts ordered rules with "first match wins", but §11.1 only exercises the five phases in a happy-path sequence and never constructs a state that matches two rules at once (e.g. review unconverged AND tasks present, which §4.3 says must resolve to review). The precedence claim is therefore stated but never pinned by an acceptance test; two engineers could implement a different tiebreak and still pass §11.1.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Add a §11.1 sub-case asserting that a multi-rule state (review-unconverged with open tasks present) resolves to `review`, exercising the first-match ordering.

finding: §4.2 defines next_action.command as `the literal next tp command to run`, but several phases have no single tp command: decompose (§4.4.2) is the agent's reasoning step `closed by tp import` (import needs a file that does not yet exist), release (§4.4.5) is `no automatic action` (human), and the dirty-tree case (§4.5.1) tells the agent to `decide per file`. What `command` holds in these cases (empty string, null, or a placeholder like `tp import <file>`) is undefined.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Define command for the no-tp-command phases explicitly — e.g. an empty string or null with the human/agent action conveyed via `summary` — and state it for decompose, release, and the dirty-tree branch.

finding: §4.4.1 says the review payload carries `the round number that will be recorded next`, but §4.5.3 surfaces a loose unrecorded round already on disk. When loose per-role NDJSON for round N exists but is not yet recorded, is the `next round number` N (record the loose round) or N+1 (a new round)? Also `the count of unresolved findings` gives no computation source — from the latest recorded round, from merged findings across all rounds, minus resolved rows in `.tp-review/`? The derivation is unspecified.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Define next-round-number as a function of recorded rounds vs loose files (e.g. = highest loose round awaiting record, else recorded+1) and pin the unresolved-findings source (which file(s), which resolution filter).

finding: §4.5.2 surfaces "a `wip` task with no recorded commit" as a resume, explicitly qualifying "with no recorded commit". The complementary case — a `wip` task WITH a recorded commit_sha/commit_shas (possible via `tp commit` on an open/wip task) — is left undefined. The qualifier implies a difference in handling that is never specified, so a tester cannot assert what the implement payload/blockers show for a wip-with-commit task.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Specify the implement-payload/blocker behavior for a wip task that already has recorded commits, or drop the "with no recorded commit" qualifier if it makes no difference.

finding: §4.5.3 / §11.4 require detecting "loose per-role NDJSON in `.tp-review/` with no matching recorded round" and naming it in the payload, but the detection is not pinned: the filename convention that identifies a per-role NDJSON, and what "matching recorded round" means (which file/marker constitutes a recorded round), are unspecified. Two engineers write different file-glob logic and disagree on whether a given `.tp-review/` layout triggers the recovery.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Pin the file-naming pattern for loose per-role NDJSON and the concrete condition (e.g. a merged/recorded round file for round N exists) that makes it "matched" vs "loose", so the §11.4 assertion is deterministic.

finding: §4.5.3 recovers `loose per-role NDJSON in .tp-review/ with no matching recorded round`, but the detection algorithm and filename convention are never given. Recorded rounds are `review-round-N.ndjson`/state.json entries; the per-role sub-agent outputs' path and naming (where the merge reads from) are not specified in this spec, so an implementer cannot tell which files count as `loose` nor how to match them against recorded rounds.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Specify the loose-file glob/naming and the matching rule (e.g. a round is unrecorded when per-role files for round N exist under .tp-review/<base>/ but state.json has no ReviewRound/AuditRound entry for N).

finding: §5.2 says `tp config --resolved` reports the resolved commit_strategy and, for auto, `the effective value detected (auto -> hc or auto -> builtin)`, but the existing --resolved format annotates each setting as `{value, source}`. Where the `auto -> hc` arrow lives is unspecified: is it the `value` string, a new `effective` field, or a note? And only `auto` gets the arrow, so the value format is conditional. Separately, `pickString` treats a non-nil pointer to `""` as present, so a task/project override of `commit_strategy:""` resolves to the empty string — which the §5.2 unknown-value rule would send to builtin+warning, while an ABSENT field resolves to auto silently; the empty-string case is never addressed.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Pin the exact --resolved JSON shape for commit_strategy (e.g. add `effective` alongside `value`/`source`, populated only for auto), and state how an explicit empty-string override is treated (unset->auto vs unknown->builtin+warning).

finding: Under an explicit `commit_strategy: hc` with `hc` absent, §5.4/§11.6 pin exit 4 for "a commit attempt", but leave the record path ambiguous: does `tp done --commit <sha>` (which only records a string, invoking no hc) also fail exit 4, or succeed exit 0? The hc flow tells the agent to close with `tp done --commit <sha>`, yet hc is absent so the sha may not have been produced. A tester cannot decide whether `tp done --commit <sha>` exits 0 or 4 in this state.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State explicitly whether `tp done --commit <sha>` succeeds under explicit-hc + hc-absent (it records a sha and performs no commit, so presumably exit 0) versus the exit-4 path reserved for `tp commit`/`--auto-commit`.

finding: §5.4 rejects `tp commit`/`--auto-commit` under an effective hc strategy with exit 2 (usage) when hc is present, but with exit 4 (state) when the strategy is hc-explicit and hc is absent. The same command, under the same commit_strategy, thus returns two different exit codes based only on whether hc happens to be on PATH — an ambiguous signal for an agent that branches on exit codes. Moreover, rejecting `tp commit` because the resolved strategy is hc is a state-based refusal (analogous to "cannot claim X: blocked", which tp maps to exit 4), not a malformed invocation (exit 2 = usage per the exit-code table).

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Use one exit code for "tp commit is unavailable under the resolved hc strategy" (exit 4/state is the closer fit to tp's convention) regardless of hc presence, and reserve exit 2 for genuinely malformed invocations. If two codes are intentional, justify why the hc-present case is usage rather than state.

finding: Every task now carries both commit_sha and commit_shas, and the spec states they are identical whenever one commit is recorded — which §6.2 makes the only builtin case and the overwhelmingly common one. tp thus emits the same SHA twice on nearly every task, and §6 defines no --compact rule for the pair (the documented --compact strip set in CLAUDE.md strips neither today).

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Emit commit_shas only when it holds >1 element (single-commit tasks keep just commit_sha; list-readers fall back to [commit_sha]), or have --compact strip commit_sha since commit_shas[0] is derivable. Either way add the choice to the documented --compact strip set.

finding: §6.1 makes commit_shas the authoritative record from which commit_sha is derived (commit_sha = commit_shas[0]), and §6.3 has reopen clear it — mirroring the managed field commit_sha, which `tp set` already rejects. But the spec never adds commit_shas to the managed-fields set. As written, `tp set <id> commit_shas=...` would be accepted, letting an agent desync commit_shas from commit_sha and bypass the tp-done recording path (violating P4: tool executes the recording, agent does not hand-edit managed state).

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State explicitly that commit_shas joins the managed-fields set rejected by `tp set` (alongside commit_sha, gate_passed_at, gate_skipped_reason, etc.), and add a test asserting `tp set <id> commit_shas=...` is rejected with the managed-field hint.

finding: §5.2 flips the built-in default to auto, and §9.1 adopts it so tp's own task-closing commits go through the hc flow (hc run + `tp done --commit <sha>`), with `tp commit` rejected under hc (§5.4). This silently supersedes the standing project convention "Task-closing commits always go through tp commit (records commit_sha) — never raw git commit" documented in CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md. The spec's migration section (§9) never states that this convention and the skill/README must be updated to describe the hc closing path, so implementers will leave a now-contradictory instruction in the repo.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Add to §9 (or §11 acceptance) that CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md/README must be updated so the task-closing convention reads "through tp commit under builtin, or hc run + tp done --commit under hc," keeping the recorded task↔commit link as the invariant rather than the literal `tp commit` call.

finding: The review and audit payloads (§4.4.1, §4.4.4) each carry 'the tp review/audit <spec> invocation that emits the round's prompts', but next_action.command (§4.2.4) is defined as 'the literal next tp command to run' — the same invocation. The command line is restated inside its own payload.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Keep the invocation only in next_action.command; let the review/audit payload carry just the next round number and unresolved-finding count — the data the command string does not already contain.

finding: §4.4's stated purpose is to embed the immediate work so a fresh agent needs no second call. The decompose (§4.4.2) and release (§4.4.5) payloads embed no such work: decompose restates the spec path (already in top-level spec, §4.2.2) plus a prose reminder that phase:'decompose' already conveys, and release is a checklist pointer with 'no automatic action'. These payloads save no round-trip and only add bytes.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: For phases with no tp sub-call to embed (decompose, release), omit payload or set it null and let phase + top-level spec carry the state; move the prose reminders into the SKILL/driver doc rather than per-call JSON.

finding: An unknown commit_strategy `resolves to builtin with a one-line stderr warning; the command exits 0` (§5.2), but `the command` is not scoped. commit_strategy is resolved by many commands (tp config, tp done, tp commit, and now the read-only tp resume). It is unclear whether every command that resolves the workflow emits the warning (noisy, and a stderr write from the `read-only` tp resume) or only tp config/commit-path commands.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Name which commands emit the unknown-value warning (e.g. only commit-issuing commands and tp config), and confirm whether the read-only tp resume warns to stderr.

finding: Message-text pinning is inconsistent. The exit-2 hc rejection hint is quoted verbatim (§5.4: "commit_strategy is hc: commit with hc, then tp done --commit <sha>") and testable, but three other operator-facing messages are left as prose only: the unknown-value stderr warning (§5.2 "one-line stderr warning"), the exit-4 install/switch hint (§5.4), and the not-clean-tree warning (§7.3, §11.3/§11.8). Tests can assert on the first but not the others; a strict test on the unpinned ones would be brittle/non-deterministic.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: Either quote each stderr message verbatim like the exit-2 hint, or state explicitly that only the presence (not the exact text) of these warnings is asserted.

finding: §6.1 says repeated `--commit a --commit b` records `["a","b"]` in order, but does not address `--commit a --commit a` (duplicate shas) or any validation. Whether duplicates are deduped, rejected, or stored verbatim, and whether shas are validated (existence/format), is unspecified — an implementer must choose.

resolved.evidence: Comprehensive round-1 revision: pinned tp resume JSON schema (fields, per-phase payload keys, blocker vocabulary); defined working-tree changes/kept classification and the keep-list mechanism (new section); resolved spec-arg vs task-file precedence and no-task-file error paths; reconciled commit-strategy exit codes 2-vs-4, bare-close, and record-needs-no-hc; commit_shas managed-field + dedup + commit_sha BC; tightened recovery/gate overclaims; doc-drift note.

suggestion: State that shas are recorded verbatim in the given order (or specify dedup/validation), so the stored list is deterministic for equal or repeated inputs.
